items.title: ACT education minister censured in Assembly as schools close for strike

children.content: ACT MLA Yvette Berry is censured in the Legislative Assembly over her record as minister for housing and education amid a teachers' strike that has closed schools across the territory.

image.altText: A child holds a sign at a rally outside.

items.title: Canberrans looking for cost-of-living relief in ACT budget might be left wanting

children.content: During practice at this Canberra football club, players discuss the ways they are saving money. But if they were looking for cost-of-living relief in today's ACT budget, they might not have found what they were looking for.

image.altText: Rach stands outside at night holding a football on the field, wearing a beanie.

items.title: Trump certainly isn't helping the ACT's homegrown budget deterioration

children.content: The US President, arguably more than the territory's own Treasurer Chris Steel, is very directly adding to the heavy weight dragging on public finances in the nation's capital. Here's how.

items.title: Commonwealth Bank pursued in High Court by shareholders

children.content: The applicants want compensation for the "inflated value of their shares attributable to the bank's wrongful failure to inform the market".

items.title: 'It's gone': ACT government says it will abolish stamp duty for first home buyers

children.content: Today's ACT budget will deliver measures to make the territory the first Australian jurisdiction to remove stamp duty for all first home buyers, the government says.

image.altText: Keys rang from lock, in a front door.

items.title: The ACT budget papers are out. Here are the five key takeaways

children.content: This year's ACT budget reveals rates will increase for most Canberra home owners, along with the territory's deficit, but the controversial health levy introduced last year will be ditched.

items.title: Aboriginal woman pepper-sprayed by prison guards during self-harm incident

children.content: The use of pepper spray on an Aboriginal woman who was self-harming after being denied access to a cultural arts program was "inappropriate and should not happen," according to the ACT's prison watchdog.

image.altText: Alexander Maconochie Centre is Canberra's only prison.

items.title: The ACT wants 26,000 new homes built in the next five years. Here's what that looks like

children.content: The ACT government describes its land release and housing supply program as "ambitious" but "achievable". So what are these 26,000 homes going to look like, and where are they going to be built?

children.content: Opposition leader Angus Taylor says the Coalition has no plan to share the seats the party targets at the next federal election with One Nation as Pauline Hanson's party says it has raised $2 million since launch.

items.title: Massive whale graveyard found in deep ocean off Australia

children.content: Hundreds of whale fossils, including those from extinct species new to science, range in age from recent to 5 million years old and are also home to undescribed deep-sea creatures.

children.content: A major Australian bank has warned the price of oil is at risk of surging to $US150 per barrel as oil inventories approach critically low levels.

items.title: Government flags risk of relying on Elon Musk's SpaceX and Starlink

items.title: Beachside towns in WA face difficult decisions as erosion gets worse

children.content: A week after Western Australia experienced one of its worst storms in decades, some stretches of coast are crumbling into the ocean at an alarming rate. Millions of dollars' worth of infrastructure is at risk heading into winter.

children.content: The pontiff has celebrated Spain's Sagrada Familia Basilica as a masterpiece of "stones, colours and light," as he marked the centenary of the death of its architect Antoni Gaudí.

items.title: Outback airship push uncertain as talks 'stall'

children.content: Giant cargo airships based in outback Queensland are being pitched as a freight solution for remote Australia, but questions remain about how the project will go ahead.

items.title: Predictive text in 'demonstrable decline' with AI-based language models

children.content: If internet chatter is to be believed, the intuitiveness and efficiency of predictive text and autocorrect have fallen off a cliff in recent years. But while researchers agree there has been a demonstrable decline in quality, the increasing sophistication of what phones can do is also making errors more prominent.

image.altText: a close up of a person holding a mobile phone and texting

children.content: The 24 Hours of Le Mans is a jewel in the motorsport crown, a bucket-list item for drivers and fans, and the 94th edition of the famous race will be held this weekend.

items.title: SA locks in AFL Gather Round until 2029 but no decision on venues

children.content: The AFL's Gather Round will stay in South Australia until 2029 under a new three-year partnership. It will include a parade and a "feature match" but no decision has been made on venues.

items.title: Brother and sister from remote SA town receive King's Birthday Honours

children.content: A brother and sister who grew up near the rural town of Lock on the Eyre Peninsula both received honours over the King's Birthday long weekend.

items.title: Bakery trains staff to assess counterfeit notes amid rise in use in SA

children.content: An Adelaide business owner who has been targeted by counterfeiters about a dozen times is warning others to be vigilant, as SA Police warn the number of incidents has increased in the state in the past year.

items.title: New species found in Australia's most 'pristine' marine parks

children.content: Researchers have catalogued dozens of new marine species found in the depths of Australia's most remote waters. The result is a window into a rarely glimpsed world.
